L. 113–128, § 475A(5)section 710 of this titlesection 796f–4 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall approve any State plan submitted under that the Administrator determines meets the requirements of , and shall disapprove any such plan that does not meet such requirements, as soon as practicable after receiving the plan. Prior to such disapproval, the Administrator shall notify the State of the intention to disapprove the plan, and shall afford such State reasonable notice and opportunity for a hearing.section 796c of this titlesection 796c of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the provisions of subsections (c) and (d) of shall apply to any State plan submitted to the Administrator under .section 727 of this titlesection 796c of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to the Secretary or the Commissioner shall be deemed to be references to the Administrator;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the State agency shall be deemed to be references to the designated State entity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to shall be deemed to be references to .section 721 of this titlesection 796c of this title

children.heading: Indicators
children.label: b Indicators
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Administrator shall develop and publish in the Federal Register indicators of minimum compliance for centers for independent living (consistent with the standards set forth in ), and indicators of minimum compliance for Statewide Independent Living Councils.July 22, 2014section 796f–4 of this title

children.heading: Reviews
children.label: 1 Reviews
children.text: The Administrator shall annually conduct onsite compliance reviews of at least 15 percent of the centers for independent living that receive funds under and shall periodically conduct such a review of each such center. The Administrator shall annually conduct onsite compliance reviews of at least one-third of the designated State units that receive funding under , and, to the extent necessary to determine the compliance of such a State unit with subsections (f) and (g) of , centers that receive funding under in such State.section 796f–1 of this titlesection 796f–2 of this titlesection 796f–2 of this titlesection 796f–2 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, carry out a review described in paragraph (1) by using employees of the Department of Health and Human Services who are knowledgeable about the provision of independent living services;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the employee of the Department of Health and Human Services with responsibility for supervising such a review shall have such knowledge; and

children.label: i
children.text: is not a government employee; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has experience in the operation of centers for independent living.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director described in shall provide to the Administrator of the Administration for Community Living and the Administrator shall include, in an annual report, information on the extent to which centers for independent living receiving funds under subpart 3 have complied with the standards and assurances set forth in . The Director may identify individual centers for independent living in the analysis contained in that information. The Director shall include in the report the results of onsite compliance reviews, identifying individual centers for independent living and other recipients of assistance under subpart 3.section 796–1 of this titlesection 796f–4 of this title

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 2 Public availability
children.text: The Director shall ensure that the report described in this subsection is made publicly available in a timely manner, including through electronic means, in order to inform the public about the administration and performance of programs under this chapter.
